title: Trigonostemon longipes
taxonomy: Trigonostemon longipes is classified as a species within the family Euphorbiaceae. This family is commonly known as the spurge family, consisting of over 2,700 species of flowering plants. Trigonostemon longipes belongs to the genus Trigonostemon, which is a group of plants native to Southeast Asia. The Euphorbiaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Trigonostemon longipes falling within the subfamily Acalyphoideae. This classification highlights the plant's unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Trigonostemon longipes, also known as Long-stalked Trigonostemon, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce symptoms of arthritis and protect against cellular damage. The bioactive constituents of Trigonostemon longipes include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Trigonostemon longipes is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions.
